name: ACER
description:
ACER

The ACER module prepares libraries in ACE format (A Compact ENDF) for the MCNP continuous-energy neutron-photon Monte Carlo code.
One of the design goals for MCNP has been to use the most detailed representation of the physics of a problem that is practical.
Therefore, the ACE format has evolved to include all the details of the ENDF representations for neutron and photon data.
However, for the sake of efficiency, the representation of data in ACE is quite different from that in ENDF.
The fundamental difference is the use of random access with pointers to the various parts of the data.
Other key differences include the use of union energy grids, equal-probability bins, and cumulative probability distributions.
tooltip: Create ACE format libraries for Monte Carlo simulations

parameters.name: nendf
parameters.help:
'nendf'

Unit for input endf tape.

parameters.name: npend
parameters.help:
'npend'

Unit for input pendf tape.

parameters.name: ngend
parameters.help:
'ngend'

Unit for input multigroup photon data.

parameters.name: nace
parameters.help:
'nace'

Unit for output ace tape.

parameters.name: ndir
parameters.help:
'ndir'

Unit for output mcnp directory.

parameters.name: iopt
parameters.help:
'iopt'

Run type for acer
  • 1 Fast data
  • 2 Thermal data
  • 3 Dosimetry data
  • 4 Photo-atomic data
  • 5 Photo-nuclear data
  • 7 Read type 1 ace files to print or edit
  • 8 Read type 2 ace files to print or edit
Use negative values (-1 to -5) for MCNPX extended format output.

parameters.name: matd
parameters.help:
'matd'

Material to be processed. Select the isotope and the MAT number will be chosen automatically.

parameters.name: tempd
parameters.help:
'tempd'

Temperature desired (Kelvin)

(default=300)

parameters.name: suff
parameters.help:
'suff'

ID suffix for zaid. Use suff < 0 to leave the old zaid unchanged.

(default=.00)

parameters.name: iprint
parameters.help:
'iprint'

Print flag

(default=max)

parameters.name: itype
parameters.help:
'itype'

ACE output type

(default=1)

parameters.name: newfor
parameters.help:
'newfor'

New cumulative angle distribution format flag:
  • 0 Old format
  • 1 New format (recommended for modern MCNP)
(default=1)

parameters.name: iopp
parameters.help:
'iopp'

Photon production option:
  • 0 No photon data
  • 1 Include detailed photon production
(default=1)

parameters.name: ismooth
parameters.help:
'ismooth'

Smoothing option for heating kerma:
  • 0 No smoothing
  • 1 Smooth heating kerma
(default=1)

parameters.name: thin1
parameters.help:
'thin1'

Start energy for thinning. Leave empty for no thinning.

parameters.name: thin2
parameters.help:
'thin2'

End energy for thinning.

parameters.name: thin3
parameters.help:
'thin3'

Thinning factor (tolerance).

parameters.name: tname
parameters.help:
'tname'

Thermal ZAID name, 6 characters max (e.g. lwtr, graph, hh2o, benz, dd2o, ouo2).

parameters.name: iza
parameters.help:
'iza'

ZA values for moderator components (e.g. 1001 for H in H2O, 6000 for graphite). Space-separated list.

parameters.name: mti
parameters.help:
'mti'

MT number for thermal inelastic scattering (e.g. 222 for H in H2O, 229 for graphite).

parameters.name: nbint
parameters.help:
'nbint'

Number of equi-probable cosine bins for inelastic scattering.

(default=16)

parameters.name: mte
parameters.help:
'mte'

MT number for thermal elastic scattering. 0 means no elastic data (e.g. 230 for graphite coherent elastic).

(default=0)

parameters.name: ielas
parameters.help:
'ielas'

Elastic type flag:
  • 0 Coherent elastic
  • 1 Incoherent elastic
(default=0)

parameters.name: nmix
parameters.help:
'nmix'

Number of atom types in mixed moderator.

(default=1)

parameters.name: emax
parameters.help:
'emax'

Maximum energy for thermal treatment (eV). Default of 1000 lets NJOY determine from data.

(default=1000.0)

parameters.name: iwt
parameters.help:
'iwt'

Bin weighting scheme:
  • 0 Variable
  • 1 Equal
  • 2 Tabulated continuous (recommended for modern MCNP)
(default=2)

parameters.name: nxtra
parameters.help:
'nxtra'

Extra iz,aw pairs for new nuclides not in the ENDF file. Enter as a list of (iz, aw) tuples where iz is the charge number and aw is the atomic weight ratio.
